title: Local (\ell = p) Galois Deformation Rings
speaker: Ashwin Iyengar
abstract: I will present joint work with V. Paškūnas and G. Böckle concerning deformation rings for mod p Galois representations of p-adic local fields. After giving a short introduction to the subject, I will explain our main result which says that framed local deformation rings are complete intersections of the “expected dimension”, and which gives a classification of their irreducible components in terms of a determinant map.

proof_stages.label: Local Galois deformation setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes a p-adic local field F, coefficients O with residue field k, a continuous residual representation [3m[0mbar rho: G_F -> GL_n(k), and the category of complete local Noetherian O-algebras over which its lifts will be studied.

proof_stages.label: Universal framed lifting ring and its geometry
proof_stages.summary: The framed lifting functor D^square_{bar rho} is defined, shown to be representable by a universal ring R^square_{bar rho} with universal representation, and related to p-adic points of the generic fiber and to complete local rings on the Emerton--Gee moduli stack.

proof_stages.label: Rank-one determinant model
proof_stages.summary: For n=1 the residual character can be twisted to the trivial one, lifts factor through the maximal pro-p abelian quotient, and local class field theory identifies the deformation ring as O[mu_{p^[3minfty[0m}(F)][[x_1,...,x_[F:Q_p]+1]], giving the finite character labels used later for determinant components.

proof_stages.label: Main theorem on local deformation rings
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts that R^square_{bar rho} is flat complete intersection of the expected relative dimension, that the determinant map is flat and controls connected and irreducible components with normality properties, and that crystalline points are Zariski dense in the rigid generic fiber.

proof_stages.label: Tangent-obstruction reduction to a dimension bound
proof_stages.summary: Classical deformation theory presents R^square_{bar rho} as a power-series ring with generators governed by framed one-cocycles and relations governed by H^2, so the Euler characteristic formula reduces complete-intersection and flatness statements to proving the matching upper bound on dimension.

proof_stages.label: Pseudorepresentation and A_gen replacement
proof_stages.summary: The proof passes from representations to Chenevier-style pseudorepresentations, factors the map from the universal pseudodeformation ring through a finite-type moduli algebra A_gen of representations with fixed universal pseudorepresentation, and recovers R^square_{bar rho} as the completed local ring at bar rho.

proof_stages.label: Special-fiber stratification and GIT dimension estimate
proof_stages.summary: The special fiber of the pseudodeformation space is stratified by reducibility/Jordan-Hölder data, known dimension formulas and the GL_n GIT quotient of A_gen are used to reduce to fiber estimates, and the essential input on singular loci of pseudodeformation strata is explained in the discussion.

examples.name: Modularity lifting/R=T motivation
examples.note: The speaker recalls the global situation where one starts from a mod-p Galois representation and proves all its p-adic lifts are automorphic, motivating why local deformation rings matter but not contributing to the local proof.
examples.engagement_reason: Modularity lifting/R=T is used only as motivation for why local deformation rings matter, with no calculation or application carried out.

examples.name: Basic coefficient rings in the deformation category
examples.note: The rings O, k, k[[x]]/(x^2), and finite power-series quotients are given to make the category of complete local Noetherian O-algebras concrete.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker defines the coefficient-ring category, gives concrete objects such as O, k, and k[x]/(x^2), and later evaluates the deformation functor on the dual numbers to identify the tangent space with cocycles.

examples.name: Trivial residual representation comparison
examples.note: In response to questions, the trivial residual representation is discussed as a special case previously approachable through an explicit pro-p presentation, illustrating the scope and novelty of the present local argument.
examples.engagement_reason: The trivial residual character case is reduced to and then computed via lifts landing in 1+m_A, factoring through the pro-p abelianization, and identifying the deformation ring using local class field theory.

examples.name: Symmetric-power functoriality question
examples.note: An audience question asks whether analogous maps using symmetric squares or cubes of Galois representations could yield statements like the determinant map, serving mainly to contrast the special tractability of the GL_1 determinant case.
examples.engagement_reason: Symmetric powers are only raised in an audience question as a possible analogue of the determinant map, and the speaker says they do not know how to analyze it.
